Ensure that documents needed to process the DV relating to the payment for the procurement of goods are completed timely to ensure the prompt payment and, consequently, the timely withholding of retention money when the supplier opted not to provide special bank guarantee as warranty;
Discuss and reiterate during the procurement stage, particularly during the pre-bid conference, the strict enforcement of the withholding of retention money. Likewise, in case of on-going or completed infrastructure projects where there was no previous withholding of retention money and for which the respective contractor still has remaining claims with the LGU, retention money shall still be withheld accordingly; and
Continue the strict enforcement of the withholding of the retention money in accordance with the provisions of 2016 R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184 for goods and infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Engineer and/or concerned agency employee mandated to supervise the implementation of the infrastructure projects to submit to the Audit Team copies of the Results of Test Analysis/Status of Quality Control Test or a valid justification for our review and evaluation.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
In case of the Construction of Evacuation Center at Sitio Ulipanan, Barangay Dahican:
Prior to being moot, issue appropriate Warning Letter to the contractor, when warranted, in accordance with the ADM guidelines; and
Timely submit to the COA the request for time extension and its supporting documents, once approved and granted by the LGU, for the validation of the reasonableness of the grounds for the issuance and the period of the requested extension of contract time for the project.
Manifest in writing the follow-up on the status of the Letter to the DILG requesting for the merging of funds and change in location of the two remaining ADM projects and take as other appropriate actions necessary to warrant positive results; and
Revisit and evaluate the LGU’s compliance with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-3 on the implementation of ADM projects. In case of inadvertently overlooked non-compliance, promptly take the appropriate actions as necessary and in accordance with the said guidelines.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Direct the MAO to prioritize the completion of the above-mentioned projects with material unutilized fund balances as soon as practicable in order to utilize these funds for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries and avoid its return to the SA for reason of non-implementation after considerable passage of time. Specifically, ensure that the following recommendations are complied with:
Manifest in writing to the appropriate unit or office the need for additional funding of Organic Backyard Salt Making Project for its implementation and utilization of the funds received from the SA;
Advise the concerned suppliers/contractors of Bagoong and Patis Making Project and Fish Processing (Tinapa/Tuyo) Phase II (2014) to hasten the submission of documents needed for the processing of their claims; and
Hasten the implementation of Sustainable Agri-Fishery Development Program-Hatchery and Phase II Fish Processing (Tinapa/Tuyo) (2015)
Ensure that the savings from the LGU counterpart relating to fully implemented BUB/GPB projects (see Annex 8) are reverted back to the general fund in accordance with the procedures laid down in Section No. 418 of the IRR of LGC;
In general, ensure LGU’s timeliness in implementing different projects funded under the different programs of the NGAs and commitment to the full utilization of these funds for their intended specific purposes for the benefit of the constituents of the municipality; and
The LGU, primarily the LCE and the LPRAT, should ensure monitoring of these BUB/GPB projects to guarantee its completion.

We recommended that Management:
Hasten the completion of the Bagong Mambulao Grand Terminal; and
Henceforth, exercise efficiency and effectiveness by achieving planned targeted results and receiving timely return of the desired benefits of projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

o Install and implement strict internal control in the granting, utilization
and liquidation of cash advances;

* Monitor the outstanding cash advances of the official and act immediately
for non-compliance;

® Require all officials concerned to immediately refund/sertle in full their
outstanding cash advances:

o For those incurred by incumbent officials, demand the immediate
settlement of their cash advances through liquidation, refund, or as
a honorarium deduction on a staggered basis;

o For those incurred by ex-officials no longer in the service of the
agency, demand the settlement of their cash advances through a
letter received personally or by their family; and

o For those incurred by deceased ex-officials, demand settlement of
their cash advances against their estate;

o Stop the practice of granting cash advances to officials unless their
previous cash advances are fully liquidated;

o Strictly comply with COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004; and

*  Enforce Section 128 of P.D. No. 1445 to erring personnel.

We recominended that management, as part of internal control and monitoring,
require the department heads accountable for the different kinds of inventories in the
table above to:

® Maintain a ledger of all incoming and outgoing supplies/goods;

e Adopt the use of RIS in each issuance of supplies; and

o Consolidate the RIS into SSMI and furnish the Municipal Accountant the
same to facilitate the timely and accurate presentation of the inventories
on hand in the financial statements.

We further recommended that management conduct physical count of all
inventories at hand at least annually and prepare the RCPI to reconcile the recorded
inventories in the books with the actual supplies'goods on hand.

We recommended that Management require the concerned officials to
immediately finish and finalize the conduct of the inventory taking of all PPEs of the
LGU and submit an accurate and completely accomplished RPCPPE duly reconciled
with the Accounting and Property Offices, in accordance with the regulations.

We recommended that management:

e Direct the Municipal Accountant with the help of related officer
concerned to review and recheck the validity and correctness of claims
Receivables. If proven uncollectible initiate a request for write- off from
the Commission on Audit pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 in
order to present the current assets reliable in the financial statements;

*  Maintain records and schedule of every receivable accounts and include
the date of recognition of the receivable the Schedule of Accounts
Receivable to facilitate the evaluation of its collectability;

®  Require the Municipal Treasurer to strengthen the collection process and
monitoring of the receivables of the municipality to avoid future loss due
o write off of uncollectible accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the validity of payable
accounts and reconcile the same with other related accounts, if possible. If the review
shows that these payables have been outstanding for more than two years and if no valid
claims against the LGU or if these are not covered by perfected contfracts on record,
these may be reverted to the general fund.

We recommended that management:

® Require the Department Heads to distinguish their GAD related activities
especially the Municipal Health Office, Municipal Social Welfare Office,
with the help of Municipal Budget Officer, to facilitate the GAD
Secretariat to formulate GPB with projects and activities equal to at least
Jive percent of the total appropriation of the municipality;

s With collaboration with the Sanggunian Bayan (SB), formulate a GAD
Code and implement it within the LGU and its barangays;

e Follow the Calendar Schedule for the preparation of GPB for LGUs and
ensure that a DILG-approved GPB is incorporated in the Annual Budget.
Also, make sure that the former will not hinder the timely preparation of
the latter; and

e Follow DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, to fully address and
implement applicable provisions in the convention on the elimination of
all forms of discrimination against women and promote gender equality
within the municipality.

We recommended that the Management:

*  Advise the members of the Local School Board, in the preparation of
School Board budget to give priority fo programs, activities and projects
enumerated in Section 272 of the Local Government Code and Joint
Circular No. 01, s. of 1998 of the DECS-DBM-DILG and therefore must
wtilize the proceeds of the SEF only for such purposes prescribed by law;
and

*  Strictly follow Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160 and item 4.0 of DECS, DBM
and DILG JC No. 01, 5. 1998 in charging expenditure to the SEF.

o Commit to the early enactment of the Annual Investment Plan of the
municipality, particlarly for 20% DF, on or before the firsi day of the

o Strengthen the planning, execution and supervision for the implementation
of the projects under 20% DF;

from other agencies; and

implementation of programs and projects to achieve the desirable socio-
economic development and as prescribed under DILG-DBM JMC No.
2011-1.
